title: senecio junodianus
intro: Senecio junodianus, a plant species native to the Canary Islands, belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is also known as Canary Island ragwort. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the liver and gallbladder, and has been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder issues. Some of the health benefits associated with Senecio junodianus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are then used to support liver health and other related conditions.
